introduction.p: Suppose that we want to build an open rectangular box (that is, without a top) that holds 15 cubic feet of volume. If we want one side of the base to be twice as long as the other, how does the amount of material required depend on the shorter side of the base? We investigate this question through the following sequence of prompts.

statement.p: Draw a labeled picture of the box. Let x represent the shorter side of the base and h the height of the box. What is the length of the longer side of the base in terms of x?

solution.p: The box has a shorter base side of length x, a longer base side of length 2x, and height h.

statement.p: Use the given volume constraint to write an equation that relates x and h, and solve the equation for h in terms of x.

solution.p: The volume is V = x \cdot 2x \cdot h = 2x^2 h. Setting V = 15: 2x^2 h = 15, so h = \dfrac{15}{2x^2}.

statement.p: Determine a formula for the surface area, S, of the box in terms of x and h.

solution.p:
The open box (no top) has: one rectangular base (2x^2), two short sides (2 \cdot xh = 2xh), and two long sides (2 \cdot 2xh = 4xh). So
S = 2x^2 + 2xh + 4xh = 2x^2 + 6xh.

statement.p: Using the constraint equation from (b) together with your work in (c), write surface area, S, as a function of the single variable x.
What type of function is S? What is its domain?

solution.p: S(x) = 2x^2 + \dfrac{45}{x} = \dfrac{2x^3 + 45}{x} is a rational function. In the physical context, x > 0 (the side length must be positive), so the domain is (0, \infty).

statement.p: Plot the function S using Desmos. What appears to be the least amount of material that can be used to construct the desired box that holds 15 cubic feet of volume?

solution.p: From a graph of S(x) = 2x^2 + \dfrac{45}{x} on (0, \infty), the minimum value of S occurs at approximately x \approx 2.24 feet, giving a minimum surface area of approximately S \approx 30.0 square feet. (The exact minimum occurs at x = \sqrt[3]{45/4}.)

With base sides x and 2x and volume constraint 2x^2 h = 15, we get h = 15/(2x^2) and surface area S(x) = 2x^2 + 45/x, a rational function on domain (0, \infty). Its minimum is approximately S \approx 30.0 sq ft at x \approx 2.24 ft.
